name: Abu Tig Marina - El Gouna
description: One of the most luxurious and beautiful marinas on the Red Sea. It is famous for its unique architecture, world-class yachts, and a vibrant promenade filled with high-end restaurants and boutiques. The atmosphere here feels like a Mediterranean harbor with a modern Egyptian twist.

name: Giftun Island
description: A paradise for snorkeling and diving lovers, located just a short boat ride from Hurghada. The island is surrounded by crystal-clear turquoise water and vibrant coral reefs filled with colorful fish. It offers several beach clubs where you can relax on white sandy beaches.

name: Hurghada Museum
description: The first archaeological museum in the Red Sea governorate, featuring a unique collection that links different Egyptian eras under the theme of Beauty and Luxury. It displays artifacts from ancient Egyptian, Coptic, and Islamic periods in a very modern and well-lit environment.

name: El Mina Masjid
description: A stunning architectural landmark located directly on the Hurghada coast. This grand mosque features marble walls, intricate carvings, and two towering minarets that light up beautifully at night. It is considered one of the most peaceful and majestic places in the city.

name: Hurghada Marina
description: The social heart of Hurghada, this modern marina offers a stylish promenade lined with palm trees and over a hundred dining and shopping options. It is the perfect place to see luxury yachts and enjoy the sea breeze in a clean and organized environment.

name: Sand City Hurghada
description: A unique open-air museum and the only one of its kind in the Middle East and Africa. It features impressive sand sculptures created by international artists, depicting historical legends and famous movie characters. The detail in the carvings is breathtaking and made entirely from sand and water.

name: Hurghada Grand Aquarium
description: Known as the "Red Sea in Glass," this is one of the largest indoor aquatic habitats in the world. It features a massive tunnel where sharks and rays swim over your head, along with a mini-zoo and a fossil museum. It offers a deep dive into the marine life of the Red Sea without getting wet.

name: Orange Bay Island
description: One of the most famous and Instagrammable spots in the Red Sea. This wooden island resort features swings in the middle of the turquoise water, bean bags on the sand, and a tropical vibe that makes you feel like you are in the Caribbean. The water is very shallow for hundreds of meters, making it a natural swimming pool.

name: Soma Bay - Sandy Beach
description: Known for having some of the most beautiful and widest sandy beaches on the Red Sea. Soma Bay is a peninsula surrounded by water from three sides, offering perfect conditions for windsurfing and kitesurfing due to the consistent breeze. The water is incredibly clear with a vibrant house reef.

name: Panorama Reef - Safaga
description: One of the most legendary diving sites in the Red Sea, located off the coast of Safaga. It is a massive coral formation with steep walls dropping into the deep blue. You can see everything here from colorful soft corals to large pelagic fish like barracudas and sometimes even sharks.

name: Paradise Island
description: A premium part of the Giftun Island, known for its wooden walkways, white sandy beaches, and luxury seating areas. It offers a more exclusive and quiet experience compared to other islands.

name: Sherry Street
description: A famous pedestrian-only street in the heart of Hurghada, lined with local shops, cafes, and international brands. It’s the perfect place to experience the local lifestyle and do some affordable shopping.

name: Central Market - Hurghada
description: The most authentic spot in Hurghada where you can see the daily life of the locals. The market is huge, clean, and well-organized, offering fresh fruits, spices, and the catch of the day from the Red Sea.

name: Saint Shenouda Coptic Orthodox Church
description: A beautiful modern church located in the Dahar area of Hurghada. It features stunning architecture with intricate icons and colorful stained glass windows that tell religious stories.

name: Zeytouna Beach
description: A unique beach located on its own island, connected by a long wooden pier that stretches into the sea. It’s famous for its calm, shallow water and its vibrant coral reef at the end of the pier.

name: Casa Cook Area
description: A high-end area known for its bohemian-chic design and luxury atmosphere. It represents the modern, artistic side of El Gouna with its earthy tones and minimalist architecture.

name: Sliders Cable Park
description: One of the largest Olympic-standard cable wakeboard parks in the world. It offers a fun and active experience for water sports lovers, where you can wakeboard using a cable system rather than a boat.

name: Sunken City - Sahl Hasheesh
description: An amazing underwater structure built to resemble an ancient Pharaonic city. It has become a home for many types of fish and corals, making it a magical spot for snorkeling right off the coast.

name: Sahl Hasheesh Promenade
description: A 12-kilometer long coastal walkway that connects the different resorts of the area. It is perfect for cycling, jogging, or a long evening walk with a direct view of the clear Red Sea water.

name: Makadi Bay Reef
description: Makadi Bay is known for having some of the most vibrant house reefs in the region. The corals start very close to the beach, allowing you to see a huge variety of marine life with very little effort.

name: Sharm El Naga Bay
description: A protected bay located south of Hurghada, famous for its untouched coral reefs and crystal-clear water. It’s a hidden gem that offers a very peaceful environment away from the city's buzz.

name: Village Road - Hurghada Promenade
description: The longest and most famous tourist promenade in Hurghada, stretching for several kilometers. It is lined with international hotels, cafes, bazaars, and nightclubs. The walkway is wide, clean, and beautifully lit at night, making it the heart of the city's nightlife.

name: El Gouna Library
description: A stunning architectural gem and a branch of the Library of Alexandria. The building is designed with beautiful arches and surrounded by a peaceful lagoon. It’s a cultural oasis that offers a quiet space for reading, research, and art exhibitions in a very modern setting.

name: Magawish Island
description: Often called the "Long Island" of Hurghada, Magawish is a stunning natural spot with incredibly calm and clear waters. It is a favorite for divers and those who want to experience a more raw and untouched version of the Red Sea islands.
